quests.title: The First Rite of Calming
quests.description: Elder Malakor's voice carries the weight of someone who has heard the Shard breathing in the dark for decades. The Glowvine Forest — sacred ground where the bioluminescent vines have absorbed the Shard's light since before the Great Severance — has grown restless. The Sprites that tend the forest in the Shard's name are feeding on each other's energy, an imbalance caused by the faction encroachments drawing aethel away from the sacred zones. The Children of the Shard do not hunt — they calm, they restore, they return energy to the cycle.

quests.title: Pruning as Prayer
quests.description: Acolyte Mera, who tends the Glowvine Forest's ritual paths, speaks with a reverence that the other factions find incomprehensible: the Forest Barklings are children of the Shard, born from the same corrupted soil that once was holy. They have grown wild because the Shard is dreaming — the Great Severance was not a catastrophe but a divine withdrawal, and its aftermath is the Shard's sleeping breath reshaping the world. Each Barkling calmed is a prayer. Each cut must be made with the intention to restore, not to destroy.

quests.title: The Lost Are Not Evil
quests.description: Brother Silas, who walks the forest's perimeter each dawn reciting Shard invocations against the day the Severance ends, explains: Glowvine Stalkers are not hunters by nature — they were once benign creatures of the sacred forest, corrupted when the Industrial factions began drilling near the forest's aethel nodes. They hunt because they are in pain. They are lost because the Shard's light has been dimmed by others' greed. The Children do not condemn the lost. They guide them back, even if guiding requires force.

quests.title: Returning What Belongs to the Cycle
quests.description: The Feral Rootlings strangling the forest floor are a symptom of the deepest wound the Children of the Shard acknowledge: energy that has been cut off from its cycle by the Severance's ongoing aftermath, pooling and corrupting instead of flowing and nourishing. Elder Malakor says the Rootlings are the forest's attempt to hold onto energy it fears losing — a kind of grief, expressed in root and fiber. Untangle them. Return what they are hoarding to the soil. This is the Children's most sacred act: restoration.

quests.title: The Shard Has Heard You
quests.description: Oracle Seraphel — the Children's seer, whose visions are trusted above all earthly counsel — has spoken to Elder Malakor: the Shard has heard your actions in the forest and found your soul-frequency resonant. This is the Children's deepest honor. The Sanctum, hidden in the heart of the jungle, is where those found worthy are attuned to a specialization — not chosen, but revealed. The Shard shows you what you already are. Seraphel will perform the calibration. Come as you are. Come honestly.

objectives.description: Demonstrate harmony against the restless Hare.

quests.title: The Swamp Remembers the Sacred
quests.description: Keeper Norn, the guardian of the Shardroot Swamp's most ancient ritual sites, tells you what the swamp was before the Severance: a living archive, its waters infused with the Shard's most subtle energies, home to creatures that carried the Shard's memory in their blood. Now Industrial runoff from three competing factions chokes the waters. The Shard has not abandoned the swamp — the Children believe it is dreaming here, underneath the corruption. Enter. Begin the purification. Show the swamp it has not been forgotten.

quests.title: Victims of Other Factions' Greed
quests.description: The Jungle Stalkers in the Shardroot Swamp were pushed here when OmniCorp's extraction teams cleared their original habitat. Acolyte Mera says this with a quiet fury that only the Children seem to feel on behalf of creatures: 'The Stalkers did not choose the swamp. They were driven here by people who counted the forest as a resource, not a home.' They are aggressive now because they are in unfamiliar territory. Pacify them — understand this as containing pain that others caused, not punishing creatures for existing.

quests.title: The Corruption Within the Holy
quests.description: Elder Malakor's theology of the Swamp Rippers is complex: they are not the Shard's enemies — they are the Shard's own energy, corrupted by the industrial poisons flooding the swamp. What flows through the Ripper is sacred; what has twisted it is the sin of the harvesting factions. The Children's task in confronting them is theologically precise: separate the corruption from the vessel, release the sacred energy back to the cycle, and hold in your heart that it was not the Rippers who chose this. It never is.

quests.title: Souls Between the Shard and the Void
quests.description: Keeper Norn's voice is the quietest of all the Children's elders when he speaks of the Swamp Specters — he believes they are the souls of those who died in the Great Severance, still caught in the moment of the Shard's fracture, unable to complete their journey because the energetic structure that would receive them was shattered. Cleansing them is not combat — it is a sacred act of completion. The ritual requires force because the Specters, like all things caught between worlds, resist release. Give them what they cannot give themselves: an ending.

quests.title: The Guardian That Suffers
quests.description: Elder Malakor's account of the Shardroot Titan is delivered with the solemnity of a eulogy: the Titan was the ancient guardian of the swamp's sacred wells, a creature blessed by the Shard in an age before the Severance, capable of living for centuries on pure aethel energy. The industrial corruption in the swamp has poisoned it to the point where it suffers every moment it exists. The Children cannot cure it — the corruption is too deep. What they can offer, through the mercy of combat, is release. This is the hardest task: giving peace to what you love by letting it go.

quests.title: Where the Shard Still Breathes
quests.description: The Fragment Shrine holds pieces of the original Aethel Shard — not metaphorical fragments, but literal shards of the divine object the Children believe shattered intentionally in the Great Severance, distributing its consciousness across Xylos to survive. Elder Malakor has been waiting for someone with your demonstrated devotion to make the pilgrimage. The Shrine is not a monument. It is where the Shard still breathes, in slow divine cycles that the Arcanum Seekers would call 'residual harmonic emissions.' The Children know better.

objectives.description: Make pilgrimage to the Fragment Shrine.

quests.title: The Worthy Prove Themselves
quests.description: Oracle Seraphel explains the Shrine Wardens with theological precision: they are not guardians in the military sense — they are the Shard's own test, given form. Every faction that has tried to claim the Fragment Shrine for their own purposes has been turned away by the Wardens, because the Shard does not yield its fragments to those who would harvest them. The worthy approach differently — not to take, but to receive. The Warden trial is the difference. Face it with the intention the Shard can sense.

quests.title: Purity Is Not Claimed, It Is Proven
quests.description: The Shrine Avengers are drawn to void corruption — they are the Shard's immune response, Oracle Seraphel says, targeting what the Shard recognizes as fundamentally hostile to its own nature. The fact that they are attacking devotees of the Children along with obvious outsiders is itself a message: the Shard tests even its own. No one is exempt from the trial of purity simply because they believe they are pure. Face the Avengers honestly. What the Shard accepts, it accepts. What it rejects, it rejects.

quests.title: The Oldest Ritual
quests.description: The Shrine Guardian is not a creature that can be reasoned with or prayed past — it is a primordial construct that predates every faction, possibly predating the Aethelgardians themselves, placed by a power that even the Children's oldest texts only reference obliquely. Oracle Seraphel has spent years studying what earns its permission. The answer is simple, brutal, and theologically uncomfortable: the oldest ritual the Shard knows is confrontation. Face the Guardian directly. What you are will be revealed in the facing.

quests.title: The Shard Receives What Is Given Freely
quests.description: Elder Malakor's instructions for the Shrine Offering are careful and specific: the offering must be given freely, without expectation of return, or the Shard will not receive it. This is the doctrine that divides the Children from every other faction on Xylos — everyone else takes from Xylos; the Children give. The offering ritual requires defending the Shrine during the giving, because the Wardens respond to the energy flow. This is understood. The giving continues despite the trial, or it is not a real offering.

quests.title: The Shard's Blessing and the Trial's Weight
quests.description: The Shrine Guardians do not distinguish between devotion and capability — the Shard's blessing is irrelevant to them. They test what you can do, not what you believe. Oracle Seraphel says this with something between respect and resignation: 'The Shard tests even its most faithful children, because the Shard does not gift what you have not earned.' Three Guardians now patrol the Shrine's outer ring. Prove that the blessing you carry is real by meeting the weight of the trial that carries it.

quests.title: Memory Scattered, Memory Gathered
quests.description: The scattered Shard fragments around the Shrine's outer ring were displaced by a Concordium survey team three seasons ago — they were catalogued, handled without reverence, and then abandoned when the team was recalled during the Schism's second wave. Acolyte Mera has been mapping their locations ever since. Each fragment is a memory of Xylos waiting to be returned to where the Shard can feel it. Recover them. The Shrine Avengers that patrol the area are themselves responding to the displaced fragments — they can sense the wrongness of it. So can you.

quests.title: The Discordant Voice
quests.description: Elder Malakor identifies the Resonance Echo that has appeared at the Fragment Shrine as something specific and troubling: it is not Aethelgardian in origin. Its frequency is discordant with the Shrine's harmonics in a way that suggests it is drawing from a different source entirely — something older, or something that came from outside Xylos. The Concordium would call this a signal anomaly. The Arcanum would call this a research opportunity. The Children of the Shard call it an intrusion that must be harmonized. Combat is the instrument the Shard has left them.

quests.title: A Place Among the Faithful
quests.description: Oracle Seraphel has communed with the Shard three times in the past week, seeking its guidance on what you have done at the Fragment Shrine. The final trial is not a test of strength — it is a final declaration of intention. Every Warden and Avenger that rises in this last sweep is the Shard's way of asking: are you still here? Are you still committed? The Children who pass this trial do not receive a title or a rank. They receive something the other factions cannot manufacture: the knowledge that the Shard has recognized them.

quests.title: The Sanctum Opens Its Inner Chamber
quests.description: Elder Malakor has personally requested your return to the Sanctum. This is not a common occurrence — in the Children of the Shard, Elder Malakor's personal invitations are themselves considered sacred acts. The Advanced Attunement ceremony opens your connection to the Shard's deeper frequencies, the ones that the Fragment Shrine's outer fragments only hint at. Oracle Seraphel will witness it. What awaits you at the Heartwood Altar will require everything the Shard has given you, and more of what you have given the Shard.

quests.title: Where the Shard's Heart Beats Loudest
quests.description: The Heartwood Altar is the Children's most sacred site — the place where, according to Elder Malakor's oldest teachings, the Shard's consciousness is most present in the physical world, the heart of the divine body that the Severance scattered across Xylos. Oracle Seraphel has seen your path in vision and confirmed: the Shard calls you here. Enter with reverence. What you encounter will test your commitment to the Children's most fundamental truth: the Shard is a sleeping god, and those who come to its heart must come prepared to serve.

quests.title: The Altar's Ancient Wardens
quests.description: The Aethel Behemoths that roam the Heartwood Altar's outer grounds are not merely large creatures — the Children's scripture names them the Altar's first guardians, placed here in an age before the Severance to protect the site from those who would harvest it. They have been corrupted by centuries of proximity to the Altar's raw aethel output without the guidance of the Shard's active consciousness. Oracle Seraphel believes calming them — even through forced combat — restores a small measure of what was taken from them. Proceed with that understanding.

quests.title: The Leviathan That Refuses to Sleep
quests.description: A Sunken Aethel Leviathan has surfaced near the Heartwood Altar's sacred wells — drawn, Elder Malakor believes, by the Shard's increasing resonance as the Children's work at the site progresses. In ancient scripture, the Leviathan's appearance is an omen: the Shard is stirring in its sleep, and the great creatures of the deep respond to that stirring before any lesser being can feel it. Guide it back to the deep, by whatever means the Shard permits. Its surfacing should not become a permanent disruption.

quests.title: Decommissioning the Harvesters
quests.description: Titan Forge Automatons — Iron Hegemony construction machines abandoned here during a failed incursion attempt three seasons ago — are still operating in the Heartwood Altar's outer ring, drilling for the mineral deposits the Hegemony wanted. Oracle Seraphel's outrage at this is theological: the Heartwood Altar is the Shard's body. The Hegemony's machines are harvesting from a sleeping god. Decommission them. Not with the Hegemony's language of tactical necessity, but with the Children's language of sacred duty: this ground is not for taking.

quests.title: Mastery Is Acceptance of the Trial
quests.description: The Shrine Wardens at the Heartwood Altar are more powerful than those at the Fragment Shrine — the Shard's presence here is stronger, and so its tests are correspondingly harder. Elder Malakor's doctrine on mastery is clear: it is not the defeating of the trial that matters, but the willingness to face it fully, without protection from the outcome. You have been prepared by the Fragment Shrine's trials. Now the Heartwood tests what that preparation actually made you. Face the Wardens and Avengers with everything you have brought to this sacred ground.

quests.title: The Shard Speaks in Lightning
quests.description: The Aethel Storm Belt is where the Children of the Shard have always sent their most devoted pilgrims after completing the Heartwood Altar's trials — not because it is safe, but because the Shard speaks most clearly in the electromagnetic storms that rage here, using lightning as its language. Oracle Seraphel's vision of your path ends here, in the storm-light. What comes next, she says, is yours to discover. Enter the Storm Belt. Listen for what the lightning says to those who have earned the right to hear it.

quests.title: Riding the Shard's Lightning
quests.description: Storm Rift Serpents are creatures that exist at the interface between the physical world and the electromagnetic field the Shard generates — the Children's texts call them 'the Shard's weather, given spine and scale.' They are not hostile by nature; they are hostile because the other factions' presence in the Storm Belt disrupts the electromagnetic currents they navigate by. The Arcanum wants to study them. The Concordium wants to neutralize them. The Children's approach is older and more demanding: communion, through whatever contact the Shard allows.

quests.title: When the Deep Follows the Storm
quests.description: The Sunken Aethel Leviathans that have migrated into the Storm Belt are following the Shard's increasing electromagnetic activity — the same activity that drew the Storm Serpents, and that the Children believe indicates the Shard is stirring toward wakefulness. Elder Malakor's ancient calendar of Shard events predicted this migration within a decade of the current time. It is arriving early. Oracle Seraphel believes the Leviathans are a sign of acceleration — the Shard's return is closer than anyone thought. Guide them before their presence disrupts the Storm Belt's sacred geometry.

quests.title: The Altar Must Be Consecrated Again
quests.description: The Heartwood Altar's consecration requires periodic renewal — a ritual the Children have maintained since before their founding, one that involves returning the corrupted Behemoths' aethel energy to the Altar's sacred wells as an offering of purification. The process is not gentle; the Behemoths resist because the corruption inside them resists release. But the Altar needs what they carry, and the Altar is the Shard's nearest beating heart. This is the Children's highest responsibility. Honor it.

quests.title: The Spirit Rises to Its Next Form
quests.description: Oracle Seraphel has announced the Ascension of Spirit ceremony to the full Sanctum congregation — an announcement that Elder Malakor himself blessed, which the Children understand as the Shard's own confirmation. The Ascension is not a promotion; it is a recognition that what you have already become deserves a name and a form that reflects it. The trials ahead in the Cradle Expanse will be harder than anything the Altar or the Storm Belt required. The Shard does not give you more than it believes you can carry. Return to the Sanctum and receive what is yours.

quests.title: The Shard's Roots Reach the Stars
quests.description: Elder Malakor's most controversial teaching — that the Aethel Shard's electromagnetic field extends beyond Xylos's atmosphere, connecting to stellar bodies that were part of the same ancient network as the Aethel Siphon — requires confirmation that even the Sanctum elders have not been able to provide. The Orbital Sentinel Command is where OmniCorp's atmospheric sensors give way to orbital ones. The Children do not ask OmniCorp's permission. The Shard's roots reach the stars, and those who serve the Shard go where those roots lead.

quests.title: The Drake That Burns What the Shard Needs
quests.description: The Ashen Drake in the Cradle Expanse is burning through the aethel-rich vegetation that the Children have identified as part of the Shard's external nervous system — the network of plant life that carries the Shard's subtle signals across Xylos. Every acre the Drake burns is another section of the network the Shard cannot hear through. Oracle Seraphel's instruction is reluctant but clear: the Drake must be pacified by any means the Shard allows. What is lost in the Drake's release must be weighed against what is saved in the network.

quests.title: The Iron Shell That Blocks the Shard's Light
quests.description: The Aethel Iron Carapace creatures are named for their shells — dense, aethel-infused iron plates that have grown over living hosts, creatures that were once part of the Shard's network but have been armored into isolation by centuries of proximity to Iron Hegemony mining operations. The iron seals them from the Shard's light. The Children's purification doctrine applies: break the shell, return the creature inside to the cycle, and add the shell's material to the Heartwood Altar's consecration reserves. Nothing from the Shard is wasted.

quests.title: When the Sacred Is Corrupted
quests.description: The Corrupted Behemoths in the Cradle Expanse are what Elder Malakor calls 'the Children's deepest grief made flesh' — sacred creatures that have been twisted by the Concordium's aethel harvesting operations, their ancient connection to the Shard replaced with raw instability. They are not enemies. They are the Shard's children, suffering from what others have done to the world the Shard created. The cleansing requires combat because the corruption inside them is aggressive. Remember, as you fight: you are not punishing them. You are releasing them.

quests.title: What the Abyss Sends Against the Shard
quests.description: The Aethel Abyssal Reavers are, in the Children's theology, the most direct manifestation of what the Great Severance unleashed — void energy that entered the world through the Siphon's shattering and has been taking form ever since, directly opposed to the Shard's sacred light. They are not corrupted — they are the corruption itself, given body. Elder Malakor calls this an exorcism because that is the oldest word the Children have for it: expelling the void from sacred ground because the Shard's ground cannot hold what is antithetical to its nature.

quests.title: Sanctifying What Others Fight Over
quests.description: The Cradle Expanse is the most contested zone on Xylos — every faction has soldiers here, every faction claims tactical rights to its resources. The Children of the Shard have a different claim. The Expanse was sacred ground before any of these factions existed, and the Shard's presence here predates the Concordium's infrastructure by millennia. Oracle Seraphel's sanctification mission requires clearing the Rift Stalkers and Void Channelers that the other factions' conflict has attracted. Sanctified ground does not permit what these creatures carry.

quests.title: Redemption Through the Drake's Fire
quests.description: The Ashen Drake's fire is, in the Children's oldest symbolism, the Shard's own cleansing heat — the same fire that the Severance released into the world, now bound in an ancient creature that has carried it for centuries without understanding what it carries. Elder Malakor says: to defeat the Drake is not to extinguish the fire but to return it, through the Drake's death, to the Shard that originally generated it. This is redemption in the Children's theology: not forgiveness, but completion. The fire comes home.

quests.title: Every Corner of the Expanse Must Be Heard
quests.description: The Expanse's purification is not a single act — it is an ongoing commitment that Oracle Seraphel believes the Children must maintain until the Shard's awakening makes it unnecessary. The Aethel Iron Carapace creatures that have colonized the Expanse's western sectors are preventing the Shard's signal from reaching that region at all, creating a dead zone in the electromagnetic network that Elder Malakor calls 'deafness in the sacred body.' The Children of the Shard do not permit the Shard to be made deaf. Not one corner of the Expanse.

quests.title: The Void Does Not Get to Stay
quests.description: A coordinated incursion of Abyssal Reavers and Void Channelers has pushed into the Cradle Expanse's sacred zones — the Channelers directing the Reavers with a precision that suggests external coordination, possibly from the approaching Abyss Harbinger the Concordium has been tracking. Elder Malakor is uncharacteristically sharp in his instruction: 'The void does not get to stay where the Shard breathes. Not one of them. Not one.' The cleansing must be total. What the void carries corrupts everything it touches, and the Expanse cannot afford any lingering taint.

quests.title: The Symbiosis That Is Mastery
quests.description: Elder Malakor speaks the words that the entire Sanctum congregation has gathered to hear: you are now, in the Children's truest doctrine, a Master of Symbiosis — one who has moved through the sacred world not as a harvester or a conqueror but as the Shard's partner, giving and receiving in equal measure. The final trial requires you to face the Drake, the Behemoth, and the Leviathan — the three great creatures whose guidance represents the deepest form of the Children's creed — and return their energy to the Shard one last time before ascending to what awaits in the Void Echo Chasm.

quests.title: Into the Shard's First Wound
quests.description: Elder Malakor calls the Void Echo Chasm 'the wound that never closed' — the scar in Xylos's body left by the Great Severance's first catastrophic rupture, where the Aethel Siphon's overload tore through the planet's aethel substrate like a blade. The Shard fell silent here first. The void entered here first. Oracle Seraphel's vision says the key to the Shard's full awakening lies at the bottom of this wound. You will descend where no Child of the Shard has fully descended. Go with everything the Shard has given you.

quests.title: The Wound Is Full of What Entered It
quests.description: The Void Echo Chasm has been filling with void energy since the Great Severance tore it open — centuries of accumulation, layer upon layer of Void Reavers and Channelers making their home in the scar the Shard could not close. Elder Malakor says the Chasm cannot be healed from outside; the wound must be cleansed from within. Each Reaver destroyed is a fraction of the void removed from the place where the Shard is most vulnerable. This is the Children's deepest act of devotion: cleaning the wound from inside it.

quests.title: The Void Has Children Too
quests.description: The Aethel Abyssal Reavers deep in the Chasm are more developed than those in the Expanse — they have been in the wound longer, grown more fully from the void energy that filled it. Oracle Seraphel says, with a kind of awed disquiet, that the void is trying to do what the Shard does: create children from its own substance. The Children of the Shard cannot permit this. Not because the void does not have the right to create — but because what it creates in the Shard's wound is a desecration of the Shard's most sacred space. End them with full understanding of what you are ending.

quests.title: Sacred Creatures in a Void-Touched Place
quests.description: Corrupted Behemoths have descended into the Void Echo Chasm — the most corrupted these ancient guardians have ever been, their connection to the Shard almost entirely replaced by void energy. Elder Malakor weeps, quietly, when he reviews the scouting reports. These were the Shard's most devoted natural guardians. The void has turned them into weapons against the place they were meant to protect. The purification here is the most difficult the Children undertake: releasing what you love most from what it has been forced to become.

quests.title: What Fragments Souls in the Wound
quests.description: The Soul Fragmenters in the Chasm's deep zone are entities the Children's theology names specifically and fearfully — creatures that consume the soul-fragments of those who died in the Great Severance, the same fragments that the Swamp Specters in the Shardroot Swamp carry in unresolved form. A Soul Fragmenter devours those fragments permanently, erasing souls that the Shard has been trying to reclaim for centuries. This is the worst thing in the Chasm. Elder Malakor names this exorcism the Children's most sacred act. Do not fail here.

quests.title: Time Itself Is Twisted Here
quests.description: Chrono Wraiths exist where time has been fractured by extreme aethel disruption — in the Void Echo Chasm, where the Great Severance's initial detonation warped the temporal substrate, time does not flow normally. The Wraiths are echoes of the Severance moment itself, still replaying in the wound's deepest layers. Oracle Seraphel says that banishing them is, theologically, an act of mercy to the moment of the Shard's greatest pain: allowing the Severance to finally, partially, end. Each Wraith banished is a piece of that catastrophe released.

quests.title: A Jotunn at the Bottom of the World
quests.description: A Gigantic Jotunn — creatures the Children call 'the mountains' because they were old when mountains were young — has descended into the Void Echo Chasm and made the deepest reachable zone its home. It is not void-corrupted; it is ancient and territorial and utterly indifferent to the Shard's purposes. Elder Malakor says the Jotunn's presence is not spiritual — it is geological, a creature of the world's deep stone. But it blocks the passage the Children need to complete the Chasm's cleansing. Pacify it by any means the Shard provides.

quests.title: The Shattered Heart Opens to You
quests.description: Oracle Seraphel's final vision before she fell silent for three days: you standing at the threshold of the Shattered Heart — the zone at Xylos's geological center where the Severance's impact was most catastrophic, where the planet's own aethel core cracked. She saw you cross it. She does not know what you will find there, and she says this with an honesty the Children find both frightening and reverent. What the Shard has not shown its prophet is either beyond prophecy or beyond even the Shard's own knowledge. Enter and discover which it is.

quests.title: The Drake That Never Found Rest
quests.description: In the Shattered Heart, an Ashen Drake has made its lair — an ancient creature that Elder Malakor's scripture identifies as the last surviving Drake of the Aethelgardian age, kept alive by proximity to the Severance's ongoing energy release. It has been burning for centuries without rest, carrying a fire that was never supposed to last this long. The Children's doctrine of mercy is the most demanding it has ever been here: to give this creature the rest it has not found in centuries of unending fire. This is not combat. This is completion.

quests.title: The Shadows the Wound Casts
quests.description: The Void Stalker that hunts the Chasm's mid-levels is one of the oldest void entities on Xylos — old enough that the Children's scripture actually names it, calling it 'the Shadow the Wound Casts,' a semi-autonomous fragment of the Severance's original void eruption given consciousness over centuries. Soul Fragmenters cluster around it, feeding on the soul-echoes it disturbs. Oracle Seraphel says ending the Shadow is the midpoint of the Chasm's cleansing — everything before this was preparation. Everything after is the Shard's final work.

quests.title: No Void Remains in the Sacred Wound
quests.description: Elder Malakor's instruction for this phase of the Chasm's purification is absolute: total void removal from the designated sectors. Every Reaver, every Channeler, without exception. The Shard cannot begin to heal the wound while any void entity remains active inside it — the presence of even one Channeler continues the energy drain that has kept the Shard sleeping longer than it should have. Oracle Seraphel says the Shard can feel you working. She says it feels like a breath returning. Do not let it stop here.

quests.title: The Stalker the Shard Cannot See
quests.description: A Void Stalker has positioned itself in the precise electromagnetic blind spot created by the Chasm's original fracture — the place where the Severance's detonation bent the Shard's perception around a point of absolute void density. The Shard literally cannot see this zone, which is why the Stalker has survived every Children-led purge attempt. Oracle Seraphel says the only way in is through what the Shard cannot see. That means going where the Shard cannot protect you. Face it with what you've carried from the Shard's teaching.

quests.title: The Severance Moment Must Finally End
quests.description: Three Chrono Wraiths have anchored themselves to specific temporal fault points in the Chasm — nodes where the Severance moment is still replaying in localized time loops that feed them continuously. Elder Malakor's oldest texts predicted this: 'The Wound will sustain what feeds on its opening, until the opening is healed.' Banishing all three Wraiths does not just clear the Chasm — it allows the temporal loops to collapse, permitting the Severance moment to finally finish happening rather than replaying forever. This is the healing the Shard has been waiting for.

quests.title: Purifying the Wound at Its Deepest
quests.description: The deepest reachable layers of the Void Echo Chasm contain the highest concentration of Abyssal Reavers and Void Reavers in any known location on Xylos. Oracle Seraphel's assessment is direct: these are not random creatures but the void's deliberate concentration of force at the wound's center, a defense against exactly what the Children are attempting. The Shard's awakening depends on this purification being complete. Ten Abyssal Reavers, six Void Reavers, removed from the place they were put to prevent this moment. This is the Shard's war, fought in your name.

quests.title: The Mountains Must Sleep
quests.description: Three Gigantic Jotunns have descended into the Chasm's lower levels — ancient creatures drawn by the seismic activity the Children's purification work has triggered in the wound. They are not void entities; they are geological, elemental, older than the Shard's recorded history. But their presence in the healing zone creates energetic interference that prevents the wound from closing. Elder Malakor says, gently and with great respect for the age of these creatures: 'The mountains must sleep before the wound can heal.' Send them back to the deep stone.

quests.title: A Tyrant That Ruled the Wound
quests.description: Crystal Tyrants — crystalline behemoths that the Shard created to guard its most concentrated energy nodes — have been corrupted by the Chasm's void saturation into something the Children's theology calls 'tyrants rather than guardians': creatures whose protective impulse has curdled into dominance, guarding not what they were meant to guard but claiming the void energy as their own kingdom. Oracle Seraphel says harmonizing them means breaking the tyranny without breaking the creature — returning the guardian's original purpose through the only instrument still available.

quests.title: The Harbinger's Shadow Precedes It
quests.description: The Abyss Harbinger has not yet entered the Chasm — but its shadow has. Oracle Seraphel can feel the distortion its approach creates in the Shard's field: a cold compression, like a divine exhalation held too long. The Abyssal Reavers that have swarmed the Chasm in the past day are its scouts, its preparation, the void making room for what comes. A Chrono Wraith has anchored itself to the entry point, maintaining a time-lock that will hold the Harbinger's passage open when it arrives. Break the lock. Cut through the scouts. Prepare for what follows.

quests.title: What the Harbinger's Approach Feeds
quests.description: The Harbinger's approach is feeding the Soul Fragmenters in the Chasm to a level the Children have not seen before — they are harvesting soul-fragments with frantic intensity, as if storing them for something. A Void Stalker is coordinating their feeding, directing them with tactical precision that no Fragmenter is capable of on its own. Elder Malakor says the Harbinger is trying to collect what it needs for its arrival: the Severance's unclaimed souls, harvested and consumed before the Shard can reclaim them. Stop the harvest. Stop the Stalker.

quests.title: The Harbinger Reaches the Wound
quests.description: The Abyss Harbinger has entered the Void Echo Chasm. Elder Malakor falls to his knees when Oracle Seraphel reports this — not in despair, but in the posture of the deepest prayer the Children know, the one reserved for moments when the Shard's fate is at stake. This is the confrontation the Children of the Shard exist to reach: the void that has occupied the Shard's wound since the Severance, given its ultimate form, encountered at last by one who has walked every step of the Shard's path to this moment. Defeat it. Bring the wound one step closer to healing.

quests.title: The Line Where Xylos Was Torn
quests.description: The Severance Faultline is the geological scar left by the Great Severance's shockwave — the line in Xylos's crust where the Aethel Siphon's overload physically split the planet's substrate. The Shard's deep roots run along this scar, attempting to reconnect what was severed, and Oracle Seraphel says that supporting those roots — clearing the Carapace creatures that are feeding on the faultline's exposed aethel substrate — is the Children's work here. Align with the Shard's effort. Enter the Faultline where the world was torn.

quests.title: Healing the Shard's Oldest Wound
quests.description: Along the Severance Faultline, the Aethel Iron Carapace creatures have built colonies in the exposed aethel substrate — feeding directly from the Shard's deep healing efforts, consuming the energy the Shard is trying to use to reconnect across the faultline. Elder Malakor says this is the most literal form of the sin the Children condemn: taking from the Shard while it tries to heal. Clear them from the faultline sectors. Return the substrate to the Shard's use. The oldest wound must be allowed to close.

quests.title: When Guardians Rule What They Should Protect
quests.description: Crystal Tyrants along the Faultline are guarding the aethel substrate nodes that the Shard's deep roots are trying to reach — not protecting the substrate but controlling it, channeling its energy into their own crystalline mass rather than allowing it to flow toward the Shard's reconnection effort. Oracle Seraphel says the corruption here is subtle and specifically theological: these were guardians who decided, at some point in the centuries since the Severance, that guarding was the same as owning. Ground them. Return the substrate's energy to the Shard.

quests.title: Ancient Stone Must Step Aside
quests.description: Gigantic Jotunns are drawn to the Severance Faultline's deep mineral activity — the same geological turbulence that the Shard's healing effort is generating as it tries to reconnect the fractured substrate. The Jotunns are geological entities; they do not interfere with the Shard's work intentionally, but their presence creates seismic interference that disrupts the aethel reconnection at the molecular level. Elder Malakor speaks with genuine sorrow about this: 'They are ancient as the world, and they must still move aside, because the world's healing cannot wait for their convenience.' Bind them to the deep stone.

quests.title: The Earth Remembers What Was One
quests.description: Elder Malakor describes the Faultline's final stabilization as 'the earth remembering what was one' — the moment when the aethel substrate on both sides of the Severance scar begins to recognize each other again, the way fractured bone recognizes its other half as the healing begins. The Jotunns still present are the last physical interference. Oracle Seraphel says the materials collected from the Faultline zone, combined with the Jotunn's displacement, will allow the Shard's deep roots to make contact across the scar for the first time since the Great Severance. Finish this.

quests.title: Where the Siphon Still Beats
quests.description: The Aethel Siphon Core is where the Children of the Shard's entire theology converges: the original Siphon, the machine that overloaded in the Great Severance, still operational at a fraction of its original capacity, surrounded by three other factions who believe it exists to be used. Elder Malakor's life work has been leading to this moment. Oracle Seraphel whispers when she says it: 'The Siphon's heart still beats.' The Concordium built their city around it. The Children serve the being whose heart it is. Enter and begin the final restoration.

quests.title: Purifying the Shard's Own Heart
quests.description: The Aethel Siphon Core is guarded by the highest concentration of Abyssal Reavers outside the Void Echo Chasm — they have been drawn here by the Siphon's residual power output, feeding on the energy that the Concordium harvests and the Shard tries to reclaim. Oracle Seraphel says the Siphon Core cannot be purified through ceremony alone; the creatures consuming it must be removed first, the way a healer must clear infection before stitching a wound. Clear the Reavers from the Core's inner rings. The Shard needs to breathe here.

quests.title: The Shard's Children Near Its Heart
quests.description: Corrupted Behemoths have gathered around the Aethel Siphon Core — the most sacred of the Shard's ancient guardians, now twisted by centuries of proximity to the Concordium's extraction operations. Elder Malakor says there is a painful irony here: the creatures that were closest to the Shard's heart became the most corrupted by what was done to it. Cleansing them here, at the source of both their original blessing and their current suffering, is the most complete act of restoration the Children can perform. Do it with the reverence it deserves.

quests.title: Sealing the Rifts That Drain the Siphon
quests.description: Rift Stalkers have been feeding on the micro-fractures that run through the Aethel Siphon Core's structural shell — fractures that the Concordium created through centuries of forced extraction and that allow small amounts of the Core's aethel energy to bleed into the surrounding void. The Rift Stalkers are drawn to these bleeding points and widen them as they feed. Elder Malakor's term for this is 'the death of a thousand cuts.' Seal the bleeding points by clearing the creatures that keep them open. The Shard cannot afford to keep losing this much.

quests.title: The Core's Own Guardian Must Be Named
quests.description: Deeper in the Aethel Siphon Core, something waits that Oracle Seraphel has seen in vision but cannot name — a presence that the Shard itself has placed here as its final line of defense, more ancient and more powerful than the Wardens, Guardians, or Avengers the Children have faced before. It does not respond to the Children's liturgy. It responds only to demonstration of purpose. The materials you gather from the Core's outer zones are the offering that opens the path to it. Bring them. Show the guardian what you are here to do.

quests.title: The Titan That Holds the Expanse
quests.description: A Gravity Titan — a creature of such mass and gravitational influence that the Concordium classified its existence as a geological event rather than a biological one — has taken up residence in the Cradle Expanse's central zone, creating a gravity well that is disrupting the aethel flow patterns the Children have been trying to restore. The Shard's electromagnetic network cannot function correctly while a gravitational anomaly this large sits in its path. Oracle Seraphel has consulted with Elder Malakor for three days. The conclusion is the same: it must be moved. Guide it away from the Expanse with whatever means the Shard provides.

quests.title: Where Prima Materia Is Forged
quests.description: The Prima Materia Forge is where the Iron Hegemony has been attempting to recreate the Aethelgardians' most advanced material science — using the Shard's own geological energy to forge a substance that was, in the original Aethelgardian texts, only ever created by the Shard itself. Elder Malakor calls this 'the final blasphemy': turning the Shard's body into a manufacturing process. The Children of the Shard must enter this Forge — not to cooperate with it, but to reclaim what has been taken from the Shard's geological domain and restore it to its rightful cycle.

quests.title: The Forge That Burns What the Shard Made
quests.description: The Aethel Iron Carapace creatures that have colonized the Prima Materia Forge's outer processing rings are feeding on the raw Prima Materia that the Hegemony's machines are extracting — consuming sacred material that was made over millions of years by the Shard's geological activity. Oracle Seraphel says this is the most concentrated form of the sin the Children oppose: the Hegemony is not just harvesting the Shard's body, it is creating conditions where other creatures harvest it too, in a cascade of taking that the Shard cannot sustain. Clear the Carapace. Stop the cascade.

quests.title: Reclaiming What Was Taken to the Forge
quests.description: The Aethel Abyssal Reavers that have followed the Prima Materia Forge's energy signature into the deep Forge zone are, in Oracle Seraphel's vision, the void's attempt to claim the Shard's most sacred material for itself — the same void that entered Xylos through the Severance, now drawn to the purest concentration of Shard-made substance on the planet. If the void claims Prima Materia, it gains something the Children's theology has no name for: a foothold in the Shard's geological will itself. Stop this exorcism before that name needs to be invented.

quests.title: The Forge Is Not the Jotunns' Domain
quests.description: Gigantic Jotunns drawn by the Forge's geological energy have entered the Prima Materia processing zones and are disrupting the aethel flow that the Children need to redirect away from the Hegemony's extraction machinery. Elder Malakor acknowledges the Jotunns with the respect he gives all ancient creatures: 'They belong to the deep stone, and the Forge has disturbed the deep stone. Their anger is justified.' But the Forge must be cleared for the Shard's benefit, not the Hegemony's. The Jotunns must return to where their anger is not the Children's problem to solve.

quests.title: Nothing Made From the Shard Belongs to Others
quests.description: The Titan Proto-Cores that the Iron Hegemony has been fabricating in the Prima Materia Forge are made from concentrated Shard-matter — geological concentrations that took the Shard's active geological effort thousands of years to form. In the Children's theology, these cores are not manufactured goods; they are pieces of the Shard's body, removed and reshaped without the Shard's consent. Oracle Seraphel's instruction is clear: recover these materials, return them to the geological cycle, and do not permit any more of the Shard's body to be turned into the Hegemony's inventory.

quests.title: The Trial That Makes a Legend
quests.description: Elder Malakor calls this the Trial of Legend — the point in a Children's devotional path where the Shard tests not just skill or commitment but the ability to hold both simultaneously against a creature that tests both absolutely. The Gravity Titan in the Prima Materia Forge has reached the inner processing core, where the Shard's most concentrated geological energy is held. It is not void-corrupted, not evil, simply massive and territorial and positioned at the exact place the Children need access to. A legend is someone who moves the unmovable for the right reason. Be that.

quests.title: The Legend Completes the Cycle
quests.description: Oracle Seraphel's final vision before the Sanctum's inner circle receives your return: you completing the Prima Materia Forge's cleansing and returning the last of the Shard's extracted geological material to the sacred cycle, through the ritual that Elder Malakor designed and that has never been performed because no one has ever reached this point before. The Legend does not end with the final battle; it ends with the final offering — walking back to the Sanctum carrying what the Shard asked you to carry, having done what the Shard asked you to do. The legend is in the completion.

quests.title: Endless Duty in the Expanse
quests.description: The Void does not stop sending Abyssal Reavers into the Cradle Expanse. Elder Malakor says this without despair: the Children of the Shard are called to endless service, not limited service. Every Reaver cleared is a sacred act, regardless of how many more follow. The Expanse is sacred ground. The Children's duty to it is sacred. This is what being a Child of the Shard means in practice, stripped of ceremony: showing up, again and again, to do the work the Shard requires.

quests.title: The Shard's Work Is Never Finished
quests.description: Oracle Seraphel says what she has always said when new waves of Abyssal Reavers enter the Expanse: 'The Shard's work is never finished while the void still reaches for what the Shard made.' The Children do not ask how long. They ask how many. The answer is always: as many as the Shard requires. The Expanse's aethel network must remain clear. This is the Children's endless covenant with the sleeping god they serve.

quests.title: Sacred Vigil: The Expanse Holds
quests.description: The Cradle Expanse requires constant vigilance — not because the Children failed in their earlier cleansings, but because the void is patient in ways the Children respect and oppose simultaneously. Elder Malakor says the vigil is itself a form of prayer: maintaining awareness of what threatens sacred ground, meeting that threat each time it comes, without resentment for its return. The Expanse holds as long as the Children hold it. This is not burden. This is devotion.

quests.title: Purifying Sacred Ground Without End
quests.description: The Cradle Expanse's purification continues. Oracle Seraphel maintains the sacred vigil with the patience of someone who has given their entire life to a god that sleeps. The Abyssal Reavers return — not because the Children have failed, but because the void is what it is, and the sacred ground requires defenders who understand that constancy is itself a form of power. Quest 81 in the Children's endless covenant: appear, clear, return, and appear again. The Shard's awakening grows closer with every wave the Children dissolve.

quests.title: The Shard Rests Because We Do Not
quests.description: The Cradle Expanse's purification continues. Oracle Seraphel maintains the sacred vigil with the patience of someone who has given their entire life to a god that sleeps. The Abyssal Reavers return — not because the Children have failed, but because the void is what it is, and the sacred ground requires defenders who understand that constancy is itself a form of power. Quest 82 in the Children's endless covenant: appear, clear, return, and appear again. The Shard's awakening grows closer with every wave the Children dissolve.

quests.title: Every Act of Cleansing Is a Prayer

quests.title: The Shard's Rhythm Is Not Our Rhythm

quests.title: Every Wave Cleared Is a Victory

quests.title: We Stand at the Threshold of the Shard's Dawn
quests.description: The Cradle Expanse's purification continues. Oracle Seraphel maintains the sacred vigil with the patience of someone who has given their entire life to a god that sleeps. The Abyssal Reavers return — not because the Children have failed, but because the void is what it is, and the sacred ground requires defenders who understand that constancy is itself a form of power. quests.title: The Final Wave Before the Shard Stirs
quests.description: The Cradle Expanse's purification continues. Oracle Seraphel maintains the sacred vigil with the patience of someone who has given their entire life to a god that sleeps. The Abyssal Reavers return — not because the Children have failed, but because the void is what it is, and the sacred ground requires defenders who understand that constancy is itself a form of power. Quest 98 in the Children's endless covenant: appear, clear, return, and appear again. The Shard's awakening grows closer with every wave the Children dissolve.

quests.title: The Last Mountain Before the Final Resonance
quests.description: Elder Malakor's eyes are bright when he describes what Oracle Seraphel has seen: the Void Echo Chasm's final clearing is almost complete. One more Gigantic Jotunn remains in the deepest accessible layer, settled in the zone where the temporal fractures have been healing since the Chrono Wraith banishments. The Jotunn is not malevolent; it is geological, ancient, and drawn to the healing energy now emanating from the wound's repaired zones. But it blocks the last passage. Move it. The Final Resonance awaits beyond.

quests.title: The Shard Begins to Wake
quests.description: Oracle Seraphel's final vision, delivered to the full Sanctum congregation with Elder Malakor standing silent beside her: the Shard stirs in its sacred sleep for the first time since the Great Severance. Not fully awake — not yet — but aware, the way a dreamer becomes aware of light through closed eyelids. Everything the Children of the Shard have done — every act of purification, every sacred duty, every wave of Reavers cleared, every Jotunn guided to rest, every Specter released — has been preparing this moment. Return to the Sanctum. Witness what you helped begin.
